findings.title: dangerous write permission on pull request event
findings.message: workflow grants pull-requests: write on a pull request-like event.
findings.remediation: Use read-only permissions for pull request workflows and move writes into trusted follow-up workflows.

findings.title: pull_request_target has write token
findings.message: pull_request_target workflow grants a dangerous write permission.
findings.remediation: Set pull_request_target workflows to read-only permissions unless a trusted non-code path requires a narrow write scope.

findings.title: pull_request_target has write token
findings.message: pull_request_target job "inspect" grants a dangerous write permission.
findings.remediation: Set pull_request_target workflows to read-only permissions unless a trusted non-code path requires a narrow write scope.

findings.title: dangerous write permission on pull request event
findings.message: job "inspect" grants pull-requests: write on a pull request-like event.
findings.remediation: Use read-only permissions for pull request workflows and move writes into trusted follow-up workflows.

findings.title: pull_request_target checks out untrusted pull request code
findings.message: pull_request_target workflow checks out untrusted pull request code.
findings.remediation: Avoid pull_request_target for untrusted code. Use pull_request with read-only permissions, or never checkout and execute the untrusted head while secrets/write token are available.
